page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent disk operations.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, while small-block cached read bandwidth is weak. Cryptographic operations and text processing perform at average levels, but database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving are weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-standard-4 offers dedicated hardware resources without the premium associated with high-memory or GPU-accelerated instances. The lack of local NVMe storage and weak multi-core scaling represent clear tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for workloads that benefit from strong single-thread performance but do not require massive parallel processing, such as light web serving, single-threaded batch processing, basic text processing, and small-scale development environments.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It contains 2 physical cores with hyperthreading and lacks local storage or GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng single-core tests. Conversely, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks, and database operations are weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth and cryptographic operations perform at average levels. Given these characteristics, the instance provides a cost-efficient option for workloads that prioritize single-thread execution over parallel processing. It is best suited for light web serving, single-threaded batch processing, text processing, and basic development environments.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is an x86_64 VM with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but weak multi-core scaling, suited for light web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 features 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for light workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual machine delivering strong single-core performance and balanced memory for light, single-threaded workloads.

4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for light web serving, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
